new_classes.label_en: Other spirituous beverages in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2208 90 78)
new_classes.label_de: Andere alkoholhaltige Getränke in Behältnissen mit einem Fassungsvermögen von ≤ 2 L (Unterposition 2208 90 78)
new_classes.definition_en: Other spirituous beverages of heading 2208 (undenatured ethyl alcohol of less than 80% vol; spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages), not elsewhere specified within subheading 2208 90, presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres. By cross-reference to subheading 2208 90 69, this covers spirituous beverages that are neither arrack, plum/pear/cherry spirit, other fruit spirit, spirits from aniseed/raki/agave/herbs/roots/sorghum/lees (2208 90 56), nor other spirits (2208 90 77), when in containers ≤ 2 L.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criteria for 2208 90 78 are: (1) it is an "other spirituous beverage" not covered by any more specific subheading within 2208 90 (i.e. not arrack, plum/pear/cherry spirit, other fruit spirit, spirits of 2208 90 56, or other spirits of 2208 90 77), and (2) it is presented in containers of capacity ≤ 2 litres. The small-container restriction (≤ 2 L via eucn:SmallContainer) is fully captured. The "other" (residual/NES) character is modelled by subclassing SpiritsBeverages2208 and noting via the complement restriction the sibling subheading 2208 90 69 (large-container counterpart). However, the full exhaustive disjointness from all other 2208 90 sibling classes (arrack, plum/pear/cherry spirit, fruit spirit 22089048, spirits 22089056, spirits 22089077) cannot be expressed concisely in a flat restriction list without a large set of complement restrictions; only the direct large-container sibling is flagged. The cross-reference to the explanatory note of 2208 90 69 provides no additional product-discriminating content beyond container size and residual character, so no further axioms are needed from that note. Coverage is therefore partial due to the inability to fully axiomatise the exhaustive exclusion of all sibling subheadings.
